text:
Once upon a time, in a quirky little town, there was a girl named Olive. She loved nothing more than collecting antique spoons. She’d spend hours wandering parks and markets, searching for treasures buried in glass jars. One particular Saturday, she visited a quaint little antique shop filled with oddities.

Inside, an elderly man with a bushy beard stroked his gray beard. He showed Olive a spoon he called "The Forest Ranger." It belonged to his grandmother, who’d loved wandering forests. Olive loved it.

As they talked, an owl hooted softly outside. The old man winked. That night, under the stars, Olive wrote him a heartfelt letter. She told him of her dreams. The owl sat silently beside her, like an ancient visitor.

When Olive went to seal the letter, she found just an owl feather. She smiled, imagining owl Owls.
